name: brand_wholesale_scan
description: Paste a brand list (e.g. all brands a wholesaler carries). Pull every ASIN for those brands, rank by profitability. Best run with the supplier's pricelist pre-loaded for actual buy_cost; without one, the engine emits max_buy_price as the negotiation ceiling.
thesis: We hold trade accounts with these brands. Scan their full Amazon catalogue.
notes: ${brands} is a placeholder — the orchestrator/CLI substitutes the actual brand list (comma-separated string OR list) before invoking the skill. Skill resolves each via Keepa autocomplete and clicks the dropdown match.

Wider seller-count window than amazon_oos_wholesale: any brand we hold trade for, we want to see the full catalogue. min 1 seller (not 2) because brand-direct sellers may legitimately be solo on a listing.
BSR ceiling deliberately wider (150k current / 120k avg) — brand catalogues include long-tail SKUs we still want to evaluate.
If the trade pricelist is supplied via --supplier flag at run time, the engine uses real buy_cost; otherwise it emits max_buy_price.
